adds.rationale: IonQ is the only NYSE-listed pure-play quantum computing hardware company with price history predating this rebalance; it fills the only named thesis wave (quantum) currently absent from the watchlist, supported by a January 2023 US manufacturing capacity announcement and March 30, 2023 Q4 2022 earnings showing ~100% YoY revenue growth and a 2023 revenue guide of $18.4-18.8M.

news_evidence.summary: IonQ announces first US dedicated quantum computing manufacturing facility in Bothell, WA, backed by a $1B planned Pacific Northwest investment; facility to house a second quantum data center.
IonQ FY2022 results: revenue $11.1M (up ~100% YoY), bookings $24.5M, cash $537.8M; 2023 revenue guidance $18.4-18.8M and bookings $38-42M.

removes.rationale: SPY is the only non-thematic ticker in the watchlist (general_markets), contributing only broad market beta with no exposure to any named wave; at the 8/8 hard cap, the non-thematic slot is the natural candidate for displacement by the quantum wave, which has zero representation.

news_evidence.summary: SVB collapse triggers a Senate Banking Committee hearing on March 28; broad S&P 500 (SPY) absorbed contagion without adding wave-specific return — generic beta provides no thematic alpha.

rationale_overall: The watchlist is full at 8/8 and covers AI (4), rockets, robotics, and nuclear — but has zero quantum exposure, which the investor names as a likely next wave to enter while cheap. IONQ is the only NYSE-listed pure-play quantum hardware company with sufficient history and, as of March 2023, shows ~100% revenue growth plus a US manufacturing buildout. The only slot to free without sacrificing a thematic holding is SPY, whose general-market beta serves no wave-specific purpose. Post-change the watchlist maps cleanly to all five named waves with no bucket exceeding the 4-ticker cap.
